item.title: Chiral Light from γ-Cyclodextrin Host–Guest Complexes
item.description: A γ-cyclodextrin host organizes two fluorescent guest molecules into a defined 2:2 complex, whose stepwise assembly leads to ordered stacking and circularly polarized light emission

item.title: Aggregation-Induced Emission Engineering within Capsules
item.description: (Co-)encapsulation of aggregation-induced emission (AIE)-active dyes in capsules enables bright green, yellow, and pure white emission in 100% water

item.title: Teaching a Simple Achiral Catalyst How to Become Chiral
item.description: N. Yalamanchili discusses how the self-assembly of simple catalysts into a chiral environment enables efficient deracemization of racemic mixtures, avoiding the need to synthesize complex chiral catalysts

item.title: π-Extended Salphen Scaffolds Enable CO₂ Electroreduction and Singlet Oxygen Generation
item.description: Over the years, salen ligands have been among the most versatile platforms in coordination chemistry, serving as efficient supports for transition-metal catalysts thanks to their modular donor framework and structural tunability. Salen-type ligands are tetradentate Schiff-base ligands, typically derived from salicylaldehyde and ethylenediamine, that use two nitrogen and two oxygen donor atoms to bind metal […]

item.title: Stanislao Cannizzaro: The Chemist Who Brought Order to Atomic Weights
item.description: 200th birthday of Stanislao Cannizzaro

item.title: Ronny Neumann Receives the 2026 IUPAC–Soong Prize for Sustainable Chemistry
item.description: Honored for pioneering contributions to green and sustainable chemistry and his leadership in developing low-voltage electrocatalytic ammonia synthesis from water and air

item.title: Cyclo‑Polyproline Macrocycles: All‑Peptide Hosts for Adaptive Supramolecular Chemistry
item.description: Aniello Palma, University College Dublin, discusses the first cyclo‑polyproline macrocycle capable of induced‑fit host–guest recognition

item.title: Highly Efficient Blue Electroluminescence from D–π–A Pyrene–Imidazole Emitters
item.description: Comparing para- and meta-linked pyrene–imidazole D–π–A isomers to determine how linkage position controls excited-state character and exciton dynamics

item.title: Light-Powered MOF Turns Flue Gas CO₂ into CO in One Step
item.description: New iron-based metal–organic framework (MOF) mimics enzyme-like pockets to capture dilute CO₂ and photochemically reduce it to CO in a single step

item.title: Snap-Together Peptides Transform Obesity Medicine
item.description: A polyethylene glycol (PEG) platform snaps peptide fragments together like Lego, making it unnecessary to rebuild complex obesity drugs each time

item.title: Gels That Glow and Flow
item.description: Designer amino acids turn simple peptide gels into smart, glowing, responsive materials for future drug delivery and bioelectronics

item.title: Halogen-Tuned Organic Afterglow for Light-Based Data Storage
item.description: How swapping different halogen atoms on an indolocarbazole core finely tunes long-lived organic afterglow, enabling color- and mode-switchable light emission for information storage

item.title: Single-Carbon Aziridination of Alkenes via Energy Transfer Catalysis
item.description: F. Paulus and F. Glorius, Germany, discuss a visible-light, metal-free method that enables regio-unconventional access to densely substituted NH-aziridines from alkenes, complementing classical approaches based on (formal) nitrene transfer

item.title: Fast MOF Synthesis with an Alternating Electric Field
item.description: An alternating electric field is used to “pre‑activate” MOF precursor solutions, cutting synthesis times to minutes while keeping structure and performance intact

item.title: Steering Tetrazine into a New Triazole Ring
item.description: A nitroimine-tetrazine reacts normally with ammonia and hydrazine but, with hydroxylamine, its cation actively redirects the chemistry to form a new triazole ring

item.title: PFAS-Free Outdoor Textiles: Plasma-Coated Organosilicon Alternatives
item.description: Developing an industrial plasma-assisted coating process for textile yarns

item.title: IUPAC’s Vision for a Data Driven, Sustainable Future
item.description: In this interview, Mary Garson speaks about IUPAC’s evolving role in data standards, ethical AI, and international collaboration

item.title: Synthesis, Functionalization, and Properties of 3-Aminosydnones: A Forgotten Class of Mesoionic Heterocycles
item.description: Synthesis and reactivity of 3-aminosydnones in cycloaddition reactions with alkynes

item.title: Dual-Mediation Strategy Boosts Rubrene Electrochemiluminescence in Water
item.description: A supramolecular co-assembly strategy enhances rubrene-based electrochemiluminescence by jointly optimizing redox chemistry and exciton dynamics for aqueous bioanalytical applications

item.title: Manfred T. Reetz (1943–2026)
item.description: Eminent German organic chemist and biocatalysis researcher passed away

item.title: Tuning Zn–ZSM‑5 Acidity for Selective CO₂‑to‑Gasoline Conversion
item.description: Directly converting CO₂ into liquid hydrocarbons, bypassing the conventional CO intermediate step

item.title: Iodine(III) Enables Green C–H Activation of Ethers for Allylic Ether Synthesis
item.description: A metal‑free, iodine(III)‑based method couples simple ethers with alkenes to form allylic ethers under mild, green conditions via a radical pathway

item.title: Photoredox Construction of 3D Cage Alcohols for Drug Discovery
item.description: Huan-Ming Huang, China, discusses building bicyclo[2.2.2]octanols and bicyclo[2.1.1]hexanols using visible light and bench-stable precursors, without relying on strained propellanes

item.title: Peterson Olefination: From Theory to Isolated Intermediates
item.description: Julia Landwehrmann and Berthold Hoge, Germany, discuss the first synthesis and characterization of several stable cyclic Peterson olefination intermediates reshaping our understanding of the mechanism

item.title: σ-Aromatic Bi₃ Ring Bound to Actinides
item.description: Uranium and thorium complexes made containing a three‑atom bismuth ring that behaves like an all‑metal aromatic analogue of cyclopropenium

item.title: Structure-Dependent Aggregation of N-Oxides: Implications for Functional Materials and Heme Sensing
item.description: The first systematic demonstration of N-oxide aggregation opens new avenues for functional material design and biomolecular sensing

item.title: Photoinduced Transformation from 3D Octahedral Cages to 2D Nanosheets
item.description: Mingzhao Chen, China, discusses metal–organic cages with tunable optical properties and responsive behaviors, which open up new possibilities for advanced material applications

item.title: Cu/Zn-Cooperative Ring Opening Gives Easy Access to ε-Keto Sulfones
item.description: A new protocol for their synthesis from cyclopropanols via Cu/Zn-cocatalyzed ring-opening conjugate addition of vinyl sulfones

item.title: Adamanphene Synthesis
item.description: Adamanphene combines diamond and graphene structures into a novel nanographene with unique electrochemical and photophysical properties

item.title: Polynuclear Os–Organic π-Clusters Deliver a Dramatic Boost in Nonlinear Optical (NLO) Response
item.description: Strategic horizontal and vertical coupling in osmium–organic π-clusters produces ultrahigh third-order nonlinear optical coefficients

item.title: Transition-Metal-Free Alkylation of Dichloro[2.2]paracyclophane
item.description: Stefan Bräse (Germany) and Fabrizio Fabris (Italy) discuss a method for alkylating dichloro[2.2]paracyclophane without using transition metals, offering a cleaner and more efficient route to functionalized paracyclophanes
